name: Banana Nut Bread ( a Risen Bread)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, mix 1 1/4 cups flour, 1/2 cup sugar, salt, and undissolved yeast.
2. Heat evaporated milk, water, and margarine to 120°F-130°F. Margarine does not need to melt. Add to dry ingredients; beat for 2 minutes at medium speed of the mixer.
3. Add eggs, mashed banana, and 1 cup flour; beat at high speed for 2 minutes. Stir in pecans and enough remaining flour to make stiff dough. Knead for 8 to 10 minutes. Set in a greased bowl; grease the top. Cover; let rise until doubled, about 1 hour.
4. Punch dough down. Divide into halves; shape into loaves. Place in 2 greased 8 1/2 x 4 1/2 x 2 1/2 inch loaf pans. Cover; let rise until doubled, about 1 hour.
5. Mix remaining sugar and cinnamon. Brush loaves with melted margarine; top with sugar mixture. Bake at 375°F for 25 to 35 minutes.
6. Cool.
